core_mechanism: a kinetic/statistical-mechanics (turbulence) theory of many weakly interacting modes that predicts a condensation phase transition — spontaneous accumulation of a macroscopic fraction of a conserved quantity into a single large-scale mode below a critical control-parameter (energy) density, with the transition existing only above a critical dimensionality and made subcritical (discontinuous) by the interaction
brief_reason: The abstract builds a thermodynamic wave-turbulence description with an ultraviolet cutoff that yields an equilibrium phase transition to a large-scale coherent mode below a critical energy density in 3D but not 2D, with nonlinear interaction rendering the transition subcritical.
